content: Hey, I'm feeling really burnt out from working on this UX research project for the past 6 months. I've been putting in 12-hour days, 5 days a week.
I'm so sorry to hear that. Can you tell me a bit more about the project and what's been causing you stress?

Well, we're trying to launch a new product by the end of Q2, and I'm the lead researcher on the project. I've been working with our team in the Bay Area, but I recently moved to Phoenix, AZ to work remotely.
That sounds like a big change. How are you finding the transition to working remotely, and do you have any concerns about meeting the deadline?

It's been an adjustment, but I'm enjoying the flexibility. I've been able to focus on my DJing hobby more, which has been a great stress reliever. I'm actually thinking of performing at a local coffee shop on Saturday, March 18th.
That sounds like a great way to unwind. Have you found any good coffee shops in Phoenix that you'd like to perform at?
Yeah, there's a great one called 'The Daily Grind' that I've been going to. They have an amazing selection of coffee beans, and the owner, Rachel, is really supportive of local artists.
I'll have to look that up. What kind of music do you usually play when you DJ, and do you have a set list planned for the performance?

I usually play a mix of electronic and hip-hop music. I'm thinking of playing some of my favorite tracks by Daft Punk and Kendrick Lamar. I've also been practicing my tennis serve, and I'm hoping to play a match with my friend, Alex, on Sunday.
It sounds like you have a lot of fun hobbies. Do you find that playing tennis and DJing help you come up with new ideas for your UX research project?

Definitely. Taking breaks to focus on my hobbies helps me clear my mind and come back to the project with a fresh perspective. I've also been trying to get into archery, which has been a great way to challenge myself and learn something new.
That's really interesting. I've never met anyone who is into archery. Have you found any good archery ranges in Phoenix?
Yeah, there's a great range called 'Bullseye Archery' that I've been going to. The owner, Mike, is really knowledgeable and has been teaching me a lot about the sport.
I'll have to look that up. It's been great chatting with you, and I hope your DJ performance and tennis match go well this weekend.
